title: Beach process moment and living-world phase
summary: Beach is the only biome without a habitat process moment. Every other biome has one authored process cue tied to world-state. This wave adds one compact tide-line wrack process during late marine-haze revisits, using the existing process system and renderer seam to complete living-world coverage.

execution_order.why_now: Beach is the only biome missing a living-world process cue.
Completing process coverage makes every biome feel alive on revisit.
Process moments drive replay and must be science-safe.

guardrails: Process moment must be science-accurate and observable by kids.
Should tie to existing beach world-state (marine-haze weather or dawn/dusk day-part).
Must follow the existing processMoments structure keyed to biome + zone + weather + phenology.
Keep it render-first and visual, not text-heavy.
Do not add a new habitat-process style for this first beach parity pass.
Do not widen this pass into route-board or notebook rewrites.
